name: José Miguel Fernández
headline: Full-Stack Developer | Backend Foundations, Web Products, Automation
tagline: Full-stack developer with strong backend foundations, building simple, reliable and useful software.
focus: Backend systems, full-stack delivery, automation and practical web products
availability: Open to freelance projects, collaborations and software engineering opportunities.
summary: I build websites, tools, automations and backend systems that are simple to use, reliable in practice and easy to maintain.
My strongest base is backend development with Java, Spring Boot and production systems, but I can move across the stack when a product needs it.

focusAreas.title: Backend Systems
focusAreas.description: APIs, services, integrations and data workflows built with reliability and maintainability in mind.

focusAreas.title: Delivery and Infrastructure
focusAreas.description: Release workflows, containers, CI/CD and deployment practices that help software move safely from code to production.

focusAreas.title: Web Products and Tools
focusAreas.description: Useful interfaces, internal tools and automations that reduce manual work and make workflows easier to run.

focusAreas.title: Distributed Systems
focusAreas.description: Services, queues and event-driven flows that communicate clearly and remain observable in production.

experience.description: Contributing to large-scale cloud-native backend systems for The Workshop, working with cross-functional teams in a security-conscious production environment.
highlights: Develop and maintain backend services in Java and Spring Boot, with a focus on clear architecture, DDD and maintainable code.
Design and implement REST APIs and internal integrations with an emphasis on reliability, testability and production visibility.
Work with RabbitMQ and asynchronous communication patterns to support reliable integrations in distributed systems.
Collaborate with SRE and Platform on Kubernetes-based deployments, Grafana and Kibana observability, automated health checks and operational consistency.
Contribute to CI/CD improvements through automated testing, security checks, artifact versioning and more robust deployment workflows.
Diagnose and resolve incidents in distributed systems with a focus on performance, resilience and maintainability.

experience.description: Worked across backend, frontend and infrastructure for education platforms serving multiple international markets.
highlights: Designed and implemented Docker-based infrastructure that improved environment consistency, scalability and team productivity.
Contributed to deployment and delivery improvements, supporting more efficient and reliable release flows.
Helped move the platform toward a multi-language, multi-portal structure for international growth.
Integrated payment systems and built advanced search with Elasticsearch, improving result quality and user experience.
Improved platform performance, scalability and Core Web Vitals, with positive impact on SEO and engagement.
Developed and maintained backend and frontend features with PHP, Phalcon, JavaScript, HTML and CSS, while also reinforcing code review and development standards.

education.degree: Master's Degree in Web and Mobile Technologies Programming
education.description: Graduate program focused on web and mobile development, application architecture and digital product delivery.

education.degree: Higher Technician in Cross-Platform Application Development
education.description: Advanced technical training in software development across platforms, covering Java, databases, testing, version control and application architecture.

certifications.name: Testing Spring Boot: Beginner to Guru
Master Microservices with Spring Boot, Docker and Kubernetes

items.title: Microservices Modernization Platform
items.context: International backend team modernizing a cloud platform for a product organization operating at enterprise scale.
items.summary: Backend work to modernize an enterprise platform through clearer service boundaries, secure APIs and better controlled database delivery.
items.impact: A clearer foundation for modernizing services and maintaining confidence in each release.
highlights: Contributed to a long-term migration from a Scala monolith to a cleaner and more maintainable service architecture.
Improved release confidence through secure service work, automated database delivery and contract testing.
Supported a product platform where technical clarity mattered as much as feature progress.

caseStudy.problem: Modernize an enterprise platform while the product continued to evolve and the legacy monolith still carried critical behavior.
caseStudy.constraints: System coexistence, service contracts, database changes and the need to retain confidence in every release.
caseStudy.decisions: Work through service boundaries, strengthen secure APIs, automate database delivery and use contract testing to expose incompatibilities.
caseStudy.delivered: Backend contributions across Spring Boot services, security, PostgreSQL/Liquibase, contract tests and Kubernetes delivery workflows.
caseStudy.outcome: The platform moved toward better defined services, controlled database changes and a release workflow with more verification points.

items.title: Real-Time WebSocket Gateway Service
items.context: Backend proof of concept for real-time communication between digital clients and internal services inside an enterprise platform.
items.summary: Backend PoC for managing WebSocket connections, rooms and event distribution between users and internal services through Redis and gRPC.
items.impact: A reproducible architecture for validating real-time communication between users and services.

imgStatic.alt: Technical view of the BetX and real-time WebSocket gateway architectures

highlights: Worked on WebSocket infrastructure for connecting users to rooms and keeping connection state controlled.
Contributed to Redis pub/sub integration for cross-instance broadcasting and gRPC communication with other backend services.
Helped document and validate local test flows so the service was easier to run, review and evolve.

caseStudy.problem: Validate a real-time gateway capable of maintaining WebSocket connections, organising users into rooms and distributing events to connected clients.
caseStudy.constraints: The PoC had to coordinate connection state, broadcasting across multiple instances and communication with internal services, while remaining reproducible to run and test locally.
caseStudy.decisions: Separate connection management from event exchange, use Redis pub/sub to propagate messages between instances and gRPC for internal contracts, with Micrometer observability and Kubernetes-ready delivery.
caseStudy.delivered: Contributions to Atmosphere-based WebSocket infrastructure, room and session management, Redis/gRPC integration, Spring Cloud configuration, and documented local execution and test scenarios.
caseStudy.outcome: The PoC validated connection management and event exchange across instances with a documented, repeatable local environment.

items.title: Euroinnova International Education Platform
items.context: Core digital product used to capture, convert and serve educational demand across multiple markets.
items.summary: Full-stack evolution of a live education platform supporting acquisition, search, payments and international growth.
items.impact: A platform prepared to operate across more languages, portals and markets.
highlights: Worked on lead generation, search, payment and UX-critical flows with direct product impact.
Helped evolve the platform toward a multi-language, multi-portal structure for international growth.
Balanced performance, maintainability and business delivery across a high-traffic digital product.

caseStudy.problem: Evolve an education platform that brought together acquisition, catalogue, search, conversion and payment journeys across multiple markets and portals.
caseStudy.constraints: The live product had to keep delivering business changes while adding languages, portals and volume without compromising performance or stability.
caseStudy.decisions: Work incrementally across backend and frontend, strengthen search with Elasticsearch, keep the MySQL data layer operable and use containers to reduce differences between environments.
caseStudy.delivered: Full-stack functionality across acquisition, catalogue, search and payment journeys; multi-language and multi-portal evolution; and continuous improvements to performance, user experience and maintainability.
caseStudy.outcome: The product expanded to more languages and portals while keeping its acquisition, search and payment journeys running.

items.title: EduCash Affiliate Platform
items.context: Affiliate management product supporting operational reporting, partner workflows and payment-related processes.
items.summary: Development and evolution of an internal tool for managing affiliates, reporting and operational processes related to payments.
items.impact: A clearer operational flow for managing affiliates, reporting and payments.
highlights: Contributed from initial implementation through ongoing iteration and operational adoption.
Worked on reporting, partner workflows and internal operations tooling connected to business processes.
Balanced practical usability with the realities of product, operations and growth.

caseStudy.problem: Turn affiliate management, operational tracking and payment-related processes into a coherent internal product for business teams.
caseStudy.constraints: The tool had to adapt to real workflows as operations evolved, retain trustworthy reporting data and remain practical for non-technical users.
caseStudy.decisions: Model workflows around partners and operational states, centralise reporting and frequent tasks, and iterate the interface alongside daily use instead of overdesigning a fixed first release.
caseStudy.delivered: Contributions from initial implementation through product evolution, including affiliate workflows, reporting, payment-process support and internal tooling for operations and growth.
caseStudy.outcome: Teams gained one product for tracking affiliate activity, reviewing reports and completing frequent operational tasks.

items.title: Betx
items.context: A commission to move a gambling-sector workflow into a backend tool operated from the terminal.
items.summary: A terminal-based Java application with local persistence and HTTP integrations for managing betting workflows without a complex interface.
items.impact: A direct operational workflow for reviewing and updating bets from the terminal.

imgStatic.alt: Technical view of the BetX and real-time WebSocket gateway architectures

highlights: Designed a Picocli command-line interface to run the main workflow from the terminal without depending on a web UI.
Used betx.yml as the local configuration point for keeping credentials, endpoints and runtime settings outside the code.
Separated SQLite persistence, CLI commands and HTTP integrations so the backend stays easier to test and evolve.

caseStudy.problem: The client needed to record bets, odds, statuses and results in a structured way without depending on a web interface or complex storage.
caseStudy.constraints: Local use, configuration outside code, lightweight persistence and clear separation between terminal, data and HTTP integrations.
caseStudy.decisions: Use Picocli for the workflow, YAML for configuration and SQLite for simple operation, with explicit adapter boundaries.
caseStudy.delivered: A working CLI, declarative configuration, local persistence, HTTP clients and tests around the primary flow.
caseStudy.outcome: The client received a lightweight tool for running the main workflow from the terminal, with local data and configuration kept outside the code.

items.title: CV Studio
items.context: A professional-tool commission focused on simplifying CV editing and document generation.
items.summary: A CV editor with structured data, validation, reusable templates, synchronized preview and PDF export.
items.impact: A simpler process for editing a CV and producing a consistent document.

imgHover.alt: CV Studio live preview and PDF export

highlights: Built a real-time editor with synchronized preview over structured JSON data and reusable templates.
Implemented ATS-friendly PDF export with a strong focus on layout consistency and document quality.
Combined product polish with typed validation and document-generation architecture.

caseStudy.problem: The client needed users to edit their CV information and see the result without working directly in a rigid template.
caseStudy.constraints: Form-preview synchronization, typed validation and PDF generation with stable layout.
caseStudy.decisions: Separate the CV model, editing flow, templates and export so each area can evolve without mixing responsibilities.
caseStudy.delivered: Live editor, synchronized preview, validated data, reusable templates and ATS-oriented PDF export.
caseStudy.outcome: The client received a clear editor that keeps CV data, live preview and final document export synchronized.

items.title: GetYourTicket · Ticketing platform
items.context: The commission started from Attendize, an open-source ticketing codebase that had gone unmaintained and needed to be made operational again.
items.summary: Recovery and production launch of an Attendize-based ticketing platform with Docker, VPS hosting, HTTPS, Stripe and a registration flow adapted for event managers.
items.impact: More than 10 events managed, each with over 1,000 attendees.

imgStatic.alt: Editorial cover for GetYourTicket, a ticket sales and event management platform

highlights: Recovered an unmaintained codebase by fixing errors that prevented the platform from operating normally.
Set up Docker, the VPS and the HTTPS certificate with Certbot to get the system running in production.
Adapted registration for new nightclub and event managers and integrated payments with Stripe.
Continue to maintain the platform and resolve issues as new events are added.

caseStudy.problem: The client needed their own ticket sales and event management platform, but the chosen Attendize codebase had gone unmaintained and was too broken to launch in production.
caseStudy.constraints: The existing project had to be recovered, its ticketing functionality preserved and the infrastructure made stable enough to handle live events and real payments.
caseStudy.decisions: Repair and adapt Attendize instead of rebuilding the whole product, containerise deployment with Docker, configure the VPS and HTTPS with Certbot, and extend registration for nightclub and event managers.
caseStudy.delivered: A repaired Attendize codebase, Docker environment, configured VPS and certificate, Stripe payments, adapted manager registration and ongoing technical maintenance.
caseStudy.outcome: GetYourTicket remains in production and has managed more than 10 events, each with over 1,000 attendees.
